ships.title: Global pull requests dashboard moves to opt-out public preview
ships.description: The global pull requests dashboard is now on by default, with an inbox, org and repo filters, saved views, and clearer review sections.

ships.title: Disable commit comments across your organization
ships.description: Organization owners can now disable commit comments across all repositories from one setting, reducing noise on old commits.

ships.title: Dependabot-based dependency graphs for Python
ships.description: Python projects now get more complete dependency graphs and SBOMs for pip, uv, and Poetry without using Actions minutes.

ships.title: Link code scanning alerts to GitHub Issues
ships.description: Code scanning alerts can now be linked to GitHub Issues so maintainers can track security remediation with their existing planning workflow.

ships.title: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
ships.description: Moderators can now classify hidden comments as Low Quality across issues, discussions, pull requests, and commits.

ships.title: New Sort by control added to Notifications
ships.description: Notifications can now be sorted newest-to-oldest or oldest-to-newest, making it easier to work through maintainer backlogs.

ships.title: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
ships.description: Maintainers can now see contributor role labels directly in public repository pull request lists before opening each PR.

ships.title: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
ships.description: Issues now show release information for linked pull requests, and project fields can set default values for new items.

ships.title: Code scanning: Batch apply security alert suggestions on pull requests
ships.description: Maintainers can now batch multiple code scanning fixes into one pull request commit, reducing review and remediation time.

ships.title: Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem
ships.description: Dependabot can now monitor Nix flakes and open version update pull requests for outdated flake inputs.

ships.title: npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI
ships.description: npm maintainers publishing from CircleCI can now use trusted publishing with OIDC instead of storing long-lived credentials.

ships.title: Dependabot now supports Xcode projects using SwiftPM with .xcodeproj manifests
ships.description: Dependabot can now detect and update Swift package dependencies in Xcode projects without a top-level Package.swift file.

ships.title: View code and comments side-by-side in pull request Files changed page
ships.description: Pull request reviewers can now keep comments, overview, merge status, and alerts open alongside the diff.

ships.title: Dependabot now detects malware in npm dependencies
ships.description: Dependabot can now alert maintainers when repositories depend on npm packages with known malicious versions.

ships.title: Issue fields: Structured issue metadata is in public preview
ships.description: Issue fields add typed, organization-wide metadata for priority, effort, dates, and other issue triage data.

ships.title: Dependabot now supports pre-commit hooks
ships.description: Dependabot can now update pre-commit hooks from .pre-commit-config.yaml using the same workflow as other dependency updates.

ships.title: Quick access to merge status in pull requests is in public preview
ships.description: Pull requests now show merge readiness at the top of the page so maintainers can spot blockers and missing approvals faster.

ships.title: Hierarchy view improvements and file uploads in issue forms
ships.description: Issue forms now support file uploads, and the hierarchy view for sub-issues has been improved with better navigation and visibility.

ships.title: Improved search for GitHub Issues is now generally available
ships.description: Natural language issue search is now generally available across GitHub Issues and accessible through the API.

ships.title: GitHub Copilot CLI is now generally available
ships.description: Copilot CLI brings AI-powered coding assistance directly to your terminal with agentic capabilities and MCP extensibility.

ships.title: Access all pull request comments without leaving the Files Changed page
ships.description: View and navigate all PR comments inline on the Files Changed page without switching tabs.

ships.title: New repository settings for configuring pull request access
ships.description: Maintainers can now configure who has access to create and manage pull requests directly from repository settings.

ships.title: GitHub agentic workflows are now in technical preview
ships.description: Agentic workflows let AI agents autonomously plan, execute, and iterate on tasks across your repositories.

ships.title: Improved pull request Files Changed page
ships.description: The Files Changed experience has been redesigned with better performance, navigation, and review tools.

ships.title: Pinned comments on GitHub Issues
ships.description: Maintainers can now pin important comments to the top of an issue for better visibility and context.

ships.title: Faster loading for GitHub Issues
ships.description: Issues now load significantly faster with improved rendering and data fetching.

ships.title: User-defined tab width applied consistently across GitHub
ships.description: Your tab-width preference now applies everywhere code is rendered, including diffs, blame views, and embedded snippets.

ships.title: Default tab size changed from eight to four
ships.description: The default tab display width is now four spaces instead of eight, matching what most developers use in their editors.

ships.title: Contributing guidelines added to repo tab and sidebar
ships.description: Repositories with a CONTRIBUTING.md now show it in the repo tab bar and sidebar so new contributors can find it immediately.

ships.title: Clearer distinction for pull request reviewer status and approvals
ships.description: Required reviewers are now grouped at the top of the sidebar with accessible visual cues replacing color-only indicators.

ships.title: Custom headers added to Issue and PR email notifications
ships.description: Notification emails now include metadata headers for labels, assignees, milestones, and issue types for easier filtering.

ships.title: Added support for WebP images
ships.description: WebP files now display inline in issues, PRs, discussions, gists, and repository file views instead of forcing a download.

ships.title: Improved file navigation and editing in the web UI
ships.description: Files found via search can now be edited directly on the default branch, and a new button navigates back from commits.

ships.title: Improved relative/absolute time threshold in Actions workflow executions
ships.description: Workflow run timestamps now switch from relative to absolute format after one hour for easier debugging and correlation.

ships.title: One-click merge conflict resolution in the web UI
ships.description: Accept current, incoming, or both changes with a single click when resolving merge conflicts directly in the browser.

ships.title: Improved blocked users view in organization and personal settings
ships.description: The blocked users page now shows when each user was blocked, who blocked them, and supports longer descriptions.

ships.title: Removed notifications from @mentions in commit messages
ships.description: Mentions in commit messages no longer trigger notifications, reducing noise from automated and bulk commits.

ships.title: New org-level setting to control repo admin permissions for installing GitHub Apps
ships.description: Organization owners can now restrict whether repo admins can install GitHub Apps, adding a layer of governance.

ships.title: Notifications sidebar counter improved to remove spam notifications
ships.description: Spam-triggered notifications are now hidden from the feed and counter, cleaning up nearly 6 million ghost notifications.

ships.title: Block org members from requesting GitHub App installations
ships.description: Organization owners can now prevent members from submitting GitHub App installation requests.
